description: Performance: cold index time and warm resolve/localize P50/P95 latency.
notes: cold start: syntax-tier served in 17,768 ms (mode syntax, semantic_pending=1); semantic-ready after a further 71,052 ms (mode semantic, semantic_pending=0)
repo NodaTime, index mode semantic, 512 files, 14760 symbols
warm localize (25x): P50 15.7 ms, P95 22.3 ms
warm resolve (25x): P50 0.0 ms, P95 0.1 ms
warm find symbol (25x): P50 1.8 ms, P95 2.3 ms
warm impact callers+implementers (25x): P50 0.0 ms, P95 0.1 ms
warm review plan (25x): P50 106.3 ms, P95 131.1 ms
incremental re-index of src/NodaTime/IClock.cs (25x): P50 22.0 ms, P95 24.2 ms
note: timings are environment-dependent; incremental re-index updates the file's syntax rows, not cross-file semantic edges.

note: re-measured 2026-07-15 on the current environment (20-core Windows, SDK 10.0.109) after the workspace-discovery fix that selects the product solution src/NodaTime.slnx (21 projects) instead of the auxiliary build/Tools.slnx (2 projects); the prior snapshot indexed at the syntax tier. Timings are environment-dependent; the semantic pass loads via tier-1 build capture (graph-grade).

C2 capture bundle (no-restore consumer path, Polly.Core, isolated empty NUGET_PACKAGES): rehydrate `fuse index --from-capture` 6.8 s (index mode semantic, 175 files, 5881 symbols, no build); oracle-grade `fuse_check` from the bundle complog 4.0 s (known-bad edit -> CS0246 reported, grade oracle, no build); clean edit -> IsClean true. End-to-end rehydrate+check ~11 s, well inside the 60 s gate.

C2 bundle sizes per corpus repo (complog is reference-closure dominated, graph scales with symbol count/TFMs): Polly.Core complog 24 MB / graph 5.3 MB (5 proj); MediatR complog 25 MB / graph 1.1 MB (5); Newtonsoft.Json complog 21 MB / graph 28.9 MB (9); Serilog complog 26 MB / graph 6.6 MB (7). Zero secret findings across all four captures (a finding fails the capture closed).

note: C2 numbers are from a single local run on this machine (SDK 10.0.109 ambient; Polly.Core built with the isolated 10.0.301 the repo pins); producer-side capture build time is ~1.5 min per repo and is not the gated path (capture runs in CI).

G5 shared daemon RSS (NodaTime, resident on): one daemon holds the resident workspace at 109 MB; a second `fuse host` on the same root EXITS under the single-instance DaemonLock ('a daemon already serves ...; not starting a second'). So the two-process baseline (2 x 109 = 218 MB, each process holding its own resident workspace) collapses to one daemon at 109 MB, a 50 percent RSS reduction. One-truth holds by construction: exactly one resident workspace exists (the second daemon cannot start), and every client routes to it (mcp serve via RemoteResidentWorkspaceProvider over the pipe with FUSE_DAEMON, the S3 hooks via the pipe directly), so an edit the daemon observes is the one truth all clients read.

note: G5 RSS is a single local run on this machine; the resident workspace requires the tier-1 build (NodaTime warms in ~14 s per resident-latency.json). The shared-daemon delegation ships opt-in (FUSE_DAEMON); the single-instance daemon lock and idle shutdown are always on for `fuse host`.
